name: all-benchmarks-full-large-extralarge
description: Complete full-suite CARTS study. First measure single-node large-size behavior against OpenMP with thread scaling, perf, and thread-level EDT diagnostics. Then measure multinode extralarge-size scaling with and without distributed DB ownership, plus targeted counter+perf diagnostics to explain why scaling succeeds or fails.

steps.name: thread-sweep
steps.description: Primary single-node scaling study. Compare ARTS against OpenMP across a standard power-of-two thread sweep to identify the scaling knee, saturation point, and any regressions at high thread counts.

steps.name: perf-single-node
steps.description: Single-node hardware-counter study at the full-thread configuration. Use this to explain ARTS vs OpenMP timing differences through cache-reference and cache-miss behavior.

steps.name: thread-edt-single-node
steps.description: Single-node runtime-structure study at the full-thread configuration. Capture thread-level EDT activity to explain whether scaling limits come from runtime scheduling balance rather than raw kernel work.

steps.name: node-sweep-baseline
steps.description: Primary multinode baseline scaling study. Measure how the default CARTS/ARTS path scales across nodes without distributed DB ownership.

steps.name: node-sweep-distributed-db
steps.description: Primary multinode distributed-DB scaling study. Compare directly against the baseline sweep to determine whether distributed DB ownership helps at each node count.

steps.name: multinode-overhead-baseline
steps.description: Targeted multinode baseline diagnostics at representative medium and large node counts. Capture counters and perf together to explain communication, memory, and load-balance behavior behind the baseline timing curve.

steps.name: multinode-overhead-distributed-db
steps.description: Targeted multinode distributed-DB diagnostics at representative medium and large node counts. Use this paired with the baseline diagnostics to explain why distributed DB helps or hurts scaling.
